§6Nitric Acid§f

§aA strongly corrosive oxidizer — essential for explosives, rocket fuel, and ore processing!§f

§bNitric Acid§f (HNO3) is a strongly corrosive, polluting liquid (NFPA 3/0/2, OX) commonly used in explosives production and electrolysis of crystals.

§6Production§f:
§b• Chemical Plant§f (2.5s, 100 HE/t) — 500 mB §bSulfuric Acid§f + 1× §bNiter§f → 1,000 mB §eNitric Acid§f
§b• Blueprint: Air§f (10s, 5 kHE/t) — 8,000 mB §bCompressed Air§f + 2,000 mB §bWater§f → 1,000 mB §eNitric Acid§f
§b• Industrial Mixer§f — 500 mB Sulfuric Acid + Niter → 1,000 mB Nitric Acid
§b• Industrial Liquefaction§f — Niter → 750 mB Nitric Acid

§6Key Uses§f:
§b• §eTATB§f — Chemical Plant: Sour Gas + 10 mB Nitric Acid + TNT → TATB (5 kHE/t)
§b• §eSolid Rocket Fuel§f — Petrol Gas + 100 mB Nitric Acid + 2 Solid Fuel → 4 Rocket Propellant
§b• §eNitroglycerin§f — Industrial Mixer: Petrol Gas + Nitric Acid → Nitroglycerin
§b• §eChlorocalcite Solution§f — 250 mB Nitric Acid + 250 mB Water + Chlorocalcite
§b• §eBedrock Ore Cleaning§f — nitric acid is consumed in various ore processing recipes

§eTip§f: The Blueprint: Air method requires significant power (5 kHE/t) but bypasses the need for niter. Use the Chemical Plant route for smaller batches and the Air route for bulk production!
